issue_title: If loop is provided, don't do cleanup
issue_body: If a loop parameter is provided to `run()`, don't do any of the cleanup steps. In this scenario, the caller is indicating that they need to do custom things with the loop, and the loop is created elsewhere. Therefore it should be cleaned up elsewhere as well.  Also we should not shutdown the exectutor, nor cancel the currently pending tasks.  Perhaps all we do is return after `run_forever()` unblocks.

```python
from tenacity import retry

async def ticker(delay, to):
```

issue_title: Implement new user permission system based on groups
issue_body:
Django already has a group and a permission system, reuse these rather than our custom solution.

* Namespaces correspond to either user names or group names
* Users can be part of groups (multiple)
* Users can have ``groupread`` and/or ``groupwrite`` permissions, allowing them to read/write to the group namespace or to the user namespaces of other members in the group. By default these are not assigned yet.
* ``allowcopy`` and ``allowdelete`` permissions will be used for file management (issue #54)
* A new configuration setting ``creategroupnamespaces`` will automatically instruct the backend to create the group directories, provided the user also has write access

(Replaces issue #57)

issue_title: Example request
issue_body:
Hi,

I am an avid dplyr user in R and somewhat new to python. I have been looking for a dplyr-like package in python for a while when I came across dfply which looks pretty close to what I was looking for.

Please excuse if this is not quite the right forum, but I was looking for some help/request some documentation/request a feature.

My use case essentially is that I have a function that operates on single elements of a data-frame columns, e.g.

my_func(a,b)

where both a and b are single elements from columns of a data frame. I have found a stackoverflow-post that shows this for an operation on a single column only.

https://stackoverflow.com/questions/42671168/dfply-mutating-string-column-typeerror

The solution show here of using X.file.apply for the column X.file in the data-frame seems to only work when you only have a single column to operate on.

What i was essentially wondering is - how do you recommend to best use dfply in this context? Could you add some documentation on how best to use functions that don't natively understand Series objects?

E.g. could there be an "Intention" like object that takes a function that operators on several parameters, each of which is intended to be a single element from a column, "vectorizes" this function and then when passed an intention object representing a "Series", applies this appropriately?

Thanks for your help!

issue_title: ConnectionUsage.update_transmitted_message() crash on bind request
issue_body:
Hi,

I just noticed that when the bind occurs, the message['type'] is empty and makes the method update_transmitted_message() crash.

How to reproduce:
Try to bind with `Connection(..., collect_usage=True, ...)`.
Here is how I init my connection object:
```
self.connection = Connection(self.server,
                                 auto_bind=True,
                                 client_strategy=SYNC,
                                 user=username,
                                 password=password,
                                 authentication=SIMPLE,
                                 check_names=True,
                                 collect_usage=True)
```

It would crash in usage.py on line 161, at the first if:

```
 def update_transmitted_message(self, message, length):
        self.bytes_transmitted += length
        self.operations += 1
        self.messages_transmitted += 1
        if message['type'] == 'abandonRequest':      <---- crashes because message is None
            self.abandon_operations += 1
...
```

It is possible to quick fix by putting the IFs in a try/catch block, but it doesn't fix the issue that message['type'] isn't set for bind requests.

issue_title: "hinge loss" in in "loss_metric" compare to "standard/general hinge loss"
issue_body:
Standard/General hinge loss:
loss = MAX(0, L(P, P+) - L(P, P-)  + C)

Hinge loss in dlib's loss_metric implementation:
loss(postive) = MAX(0, L(P, P+) - dist_thresh + C)
loss(negtive) = MAX(0, dist_thresh - L(P, P-)  + C)
loss = loss(postive) + loss(negtive)

It seems that all "sphere's radius" in dlib's impl have the same "radius < dist_thresh" but the standard hinge loss.

I want to train a network to compare images similarity, both in the same label(johns) and in different labels(john/david/tom...).

In the same label(john1.jpg compare to johnx.jpg):
I think dlib's "hinge loss" implementation will perform better

But how it is performed in different labels(johnx.jpg compare to tomx.jpg):
Which one is better in theory?
Dose dlib's impl of "hinge loss" is a optimized one?
What's the differences?

my english is bad, sorry.
thank you and for your great dlib!

best regards.

Add PyPy support to CircleCI
issue_body:
Remnants of failed attempt:
https://github.com/dhermes/bezier/blob/a6b30013cccf8998e5b62e90a74db9f039e1b7a9/tox.ini

```
$ tox -e functional
...

...

...
$ tox -e py27

...
```

issue_title: ExpiredSignatureError when exp > utcnow
issue_body:
I'm not really sure what's going on here, but if I decode without verifying the `exp` field is quite clearly greater than current UTC:

```python
>>> jwt.decode(token, key, algorithms=['RS256'])
jwt.exceptions.ExpiredSignatureError: Signature has expired
>>> decoded = jwt.decode(token, key, algorithms=['RS256'], verify=False)
>>> datetime.fromtimestamp(decoded['exp']) > datetime.utcnow()
True
>>> with freezegun.freezetime(datetime.utcnow()):
...     jwt.decode(token, key, algorithms=['RS256'])
...
jwt.exceptions.ExpiredSignatureError: Signature has expired
```

Though it does verify if I freeze time to some time in the past:
```python
>>> with freezegun.freezetime(datetime.today().date()):
...     jwt.decode(token, key, algorithms=['RS256'])
...
{...}
```

All that I can think is that it may be because the expiry is less than 'actual' time, which is BST (UTC+1), and non-UTC system time is slipping through some how. This issue occurs on multiple (all) machines.

issue_title: v0.7/0.7.1 does not longer support long types as start/end position
issue_body:
I discovered that the following code

```
#!/usr/bin/python
import pybedtools
regions = [('chr2', long(1), long(2), 'tag')]
b = pybedtools.BedTool(regions)
```
does not work on my pybedtools 0.7.1 installation. It works fine with 0.6.9. The following works fine, too:

```
#!/usr/bin/python
import pybedtools
regions = [('chr2', int(1), int(2), 'tag')]
b = pybedtools.BedTool(regions)
```

Now I am not saying this is a bug, but it definetely crashed stuff that was working previously. In my particuar case, the start/end positions come from a database and were ``long`` by default. Running ``str()`` or ``int()`` on the values fixes _my_ problem.

issue_title: Support for `__iter__()` with loops in them
issue_body:
```
class Iter:
    def __iter__(self):
        i = 0
        while True:
            v = 1
            yield v
            i += 1
a, b = Iter()
#? int()
a
#? int()  <--- this one fails (on the linter branch)
b
```

The `__iter__` method above is a simplified version from the one in `collections.abc.Sequence`: https://hg.python.org/cpython/file/3.5/Lib/_collections_abc.py. Support for it would allow iteration support for all subclasses of `collections.abc.Sequence`.

See also: https://github.com/davidhalter/jedi/pull/663#issuecomment-172317854


Both machines are in different time zones

```sh

+0300
```

```sh

+0200
```

From looking through the freezegun code it appears the default `tz_offset` is 0, which seems fine (no special handling for undefined values). My best explanation is that freezegun doesn't handle `utcnow()` properly.

issue_title: Control over labels
issue_body:
There are cases where an application may need control over the output label where it might differ from the attribute name.

The case I am working on is a JSON-LD serializer, where labels can start with the `@` prefix: `@id`, `@type`, etc. This is not a valid attribute name in Python, but it should be reflected in the serialized output.

I've tried to get around this with custom subclasses, but adherence to the attribute name as output label is fairly deep in the functioning of the serializer. 

So what I propose is to add an (optional) label to serializer fields, `label`, that would get carried along in the `_compile_field_to_tuple` tuple. If `label` is not `None`, the `_serializer` method uses it as the key in the output instead of `name`. 

If this sounds like something you would find useful, I could put together a pull request and send it upstream.

issue_title: Any URIs with unicode characters in them throw an exception in middleware
issue_body:
Whenever a URI is processed through the **whitenoise** middleware, if there are any unicode characters in that URI, an exception is thrown and results in a 500 error if being run in a Django server. This **only occurs when the *autorefresh* setting is *True***. It seemingly only affects development environments.

For example:

```bash
$ curl -X GET http://127.0.0.1:8000/static/
```

will result in a stack trace like so, which is an uncaught exception in Django middleware, causing a 500.

```python
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File ".../lib/python2.7/wsgiref/handlers.py", line 85, in run
    self.result = application(self.environ, self.start_response)
  File ".../lib/python2.7/site-packages/whitenoise/base.py", line 57, in __call__
    static_file = self.find_file(environ['PATH_INFO'])
  File ".../lib/python2.7/site-packages/whitenoise/django.py", line 75, in find_file
    if self.use_finders and url.startswith(self.static_prefix):
UnicodeDecodeError: 'ascii' codec can't decode byte 0xe2 in position 78: ordinal not in range(128)
```

This can be reproduced on a bare Django project (I am using v1.9.6, Python 2.7.11) simply by configuring the STATIC_URL and adding the whitenoise middleware.

Looking in the library, it seems like when the **PATH_INFO** environment variable is pulled (whitenoise/base.py:57), it is left as a plain byte string instead of being decoded into a unicode object. Decoding this str object at this spot seems to fix my issue, but I don't know if other places need this as well.

It seems the crux of this error can be reproduced by the following code:
```python
>>> b""
'\xef\xa3\xbf'
>>> b"".startswith(u"some text")
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
UnicodeDecodeError: 'ascii' codec can't decode byte 0xef in position 0: ordinal not in range(128)
```

``` python
        exit(1)

    print('About to garbage collect')

if __name__ == "__main__":
```

issue_title: Reading duplicate note-off events results in zero-duration notes
issue_body:
When the input MIDI file contains note-off events for already inactive notes (with note-off events given before) pretty-midi still creates note events with zero duration. Writing such notes then results in spurious long-running notes that were not present originally.

Note that in this case there were two overlapping notes: note-on, note-on, note-off, note-off.

Example: [bach-js_kunst_der_fuge_01_(c)unknown[2].mid](https://filebin.ca/3ThfIaDkkjvD/bach-js_kunst_der_fuge_01_cunknown2.mid) (source: kunstderfuge.com)

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/446124/28276344-d80f4b06-6b40-11e7-9078-2541f710987a.png)

If we zoom in the the start of the track 1 (first voice). Original looks like:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/446124/28301674-a643cc56-6bb2-11e7-87df-517e0f4f7858.png)

And written one:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/446124/28301685-affcb9c4-6bb2-11e7-9230-af3497be71b1.png)

Mido `print_track()` - original:

```
<meta message midi_port port=1 time=0>
<meta message track_name name='Contrapunctus I' time=0>
<message control_change channel=0 control=121 value=0 time=0>
<message note_on channel=0 note=69 velocity=88 time=1536>
<message note_on channel=0 note=74 velocity=88 time=192>
<message note_on channel=0 note=69 velocity=0 time=0>
<message note_on channel=0 note=72 velocity=88 time=192>
<message note_on channel=0 note=74 velocity=0 time=0>
<message note_on channel=0 note=69 velocity=88 time=192>
<message note_on channel=0 note=72 velocity=0 time=0>
<message note_on channel=0 note=68 velocity=88 time=192>
<message note_on channel=0 note=69 velocity=0 time=0>
<message note_on channel=0 note=69 velocity=88 time=192>
<message note_on channel=0 note=68 velocity=0 time=0>
<message note_on channel=0 note=71 velocity=88 time=96>
<message note_on channel=0 note=69 velocity=0 time=0>
<message note_on channel=0 note=72 velocity=88 time=96> <-- note-on
<message note_on channel=0 note=71 velocity=0 time=0>
<message note_on channel=0 note=72 velocity=88 time=192> <-- another note-on!
<message note_on channel=0 note=72 velocity=0 time=0> <-- proper note-off
<message note_on channel=0 note=74 velocity=88 time=48>
<message note_on channel=0 note=72 velocity=0 time=0> <-- duplicate note-off!
<message note_on channel=0 note=72 velocity=88 time=48>
<message note_on channel=0 note=74 velocity=0 time=0>
<message note_on channel=0 note=70 velocity=88 time=48>
<message note_on channel=0 note=72 velocity=0 time=0>
<message note_on channel=0 note=69 velocity=88 time=48>
<message note_on channel=0 note=70 velocity=0 time=0>
<message note_on channel=0 note=62 velocity=72 time=48>
<message note_on channel=0 note=69 velocity=0 time=0>
<message note_on channel=0 note=74 velocity=72 time=48>
<message note_on channel=0 note=62 velocity=0 time=0>
<message note_on channel=0 note=73 velocity=72 time=192>
<message note_on channel=0 note=74 velocity=0 time=0>
```

Mido `print_track()` - written:

```
written - bad:
<meta message track_name name='Contrapunctus I' time=0>
<message program_change channel=0 program=0 time=0>
<message control_change channel=0 control=121 value=0 time=0>
<message note_on channel=0 note=69 velocity=88 time=1536>
<message note_on channel=0 note=69 velocity=0 time=192>
<message note_on channel=0 note=74 velocity=88 time=0>
<message note_on channel=0 note=72 velocity=88 time=192>
<message note_on channel=0 note=74 velocity=0 time=0>
<message note_on channel=0 note=69 velocity=88 time=192>
<message note_on channel=0 note=72 velocity=0 time=0>
<message note_on channel=0 note=68 velocity=88 time=192>
<message note_on channel=0 note=69 velocity=0 time=0>
<message note_on channel=0 note=68 velocity=0 time=192>
<message note_on channel=0 note=69 velocity=88 time=0>
<message note_on channel=0 note=69 velocity=0 time=96>
<message note_on channel=0 note=71 velocity=88 time=0>
<message note_on channel=0 note=71 velocity=0 time=96>
<message note_on channel=0 note=72 velocity=88 time=0> <-- first note-on
<message note_on channel=0 note=72 velocity=0 time=192> <-- first note-off
<message note_on channel=0 note=72 velocity=0 time=0> <-- second note-off
<message note_on channel=0 note=72 velocity=88 time=0> <-- second note-on!
<message note_on channel=0 note=74 velocity=88 time=48>
<message note_on channel=0 note=72 velocity=88 time=48> <-- another note-on!
<message note_on channel=0 note=74 velocity=0 time=0>
<message note_on channel=0 note=70 velocity=88 time=48>
<message note_on channel=0 note=72 velocity=0 time=0> <-- note off
<message note_on channel=0 note=69 velocity=88 time=48>
<message note_on channel=0 note=70 velocity=0 time=0>
<message note_on channel=0 note=62 velocity=72 time=48>
<message note_on channel=0 note=69 velocity=0 time=0>
<message note_on channel=0 note=62 velocity=0 time=48>
<message note_on channel=0 note=74 velocity=72 time=0>
<message note_on channel=0 note=73 velocity=72 time=192>
<message note_on channel=0 note=74 velocity=0 time=0>
```

If we extract the input sequence of two notes of pitch 72:

```
<message note_on channel=0 note=72 velocity=88 time=0>
<message note_on channel=0 note=72 velocity=88 time=192>
<message note_on channel=0 note=72 velocity=0 time=0>
<message note_on channel=0 note=72 velocity=0 time=48>
```

it seems that the meaning was as follows (two consequent notes of length 192 and 48):

```
<message note_on channel=0 note=72 velocity=88 time=0>
<message note_on channel=0 note=72 velocity=0 time=192>
<message note_on channel=0 note=72 velocity=88 time=0>
<message note_on channel=0 note=72 velocity=0 time=48>
```

The only problem was that the note-off event preceded the note-on event, even though they occurred at the same time. Instead we created one note of duration 192, then one zero-duration note and one long note that was closed after encountering next note-off for that pitch.

We should fix two bugs here:

- when processing events at single time step, first close open notes, then open new notes
- ignore duplicate note-off events when reading MIDI
- remove zero-duration events in case they appear

Originally reported by: **Christian Sandberg (Bitbucket: [sandberg](https://bitbucket.org/sandberg), GitHub: [sandberg](https://github.com/sandberg))**

---

Found some inconsistencies between implementations.
1. `send()` should raise can.CanError on failed transmissions according to documentation.
- [x] a)  PCAN logs a warning and returns False
- [x] b) socketcan_native logs a warning
- [x] c)socketcan_ctypes ?

2. [x] serial does not implement `send()` and `recv()`
3. [x] socketcan_native does not implement `shutdown()`, implements `__del__` instead.
4. [x] usb2can uses argument `baud` specified in kbit/s while others use bitrate and bits/s.
5. [x] On Kvaser recv() when timeout is None, the call should block indefinitely until a message is received. Now it returns immediately.

issue_title: Integration with codecov.io
issue_body:
See codecov/support#364, in short coverxygen can be used to get doxygen coverage numbers on codecov.io by simply adding something like:
```
make doxygen
python -m coverxygen --xml-dir xml/ --src-dir . --output doxygen.coverage.info
bash <(curl -s https://codecov.io/bash) -R . -F doxygen -X gcov -f doxygen.coverage.info
```

issue_title: Cannot use with 3.7 due to async keywork being used
Umask too permissive
issue_body:
In [`sheets.py`, line 17](/chrisallenlane/cheat/blob/master/cheat/sheets.py#L17) umask is set to zero, giving permissions `0777` (a.k.a. `drwxrwxrwx`), i.e. user/group/world read-write-view. That's far too permissible. A user's umask is usually set to `0022` or `0027` (yielding `drwxr-xr-x` resp. `drwxr-x---`).

Why do you feel it's necessary to clear the umask, thus making a world writeable directory?

issue_title: Possible issue with bit fields
issue_body:
This may be an issue with me not understanding fixtures and/or SQLAlchemy, but any assistance would be great!

I have some data dumped from an MS SQL DB, which is using BIT fields for true/false.  I'm trying to load it into a PostgreSQL DB for testing.  The data is exported by getting the records (via SQLAlchemy), and then looping over them and generating each dict thus:
```
def as_dict(r):
    d = {}

    for c in r.__table__.columns:
        o = getattr(r, c.name)
        d[c.name]= o

    return d
```
That works great, properly formats UUIDs and date/times.  However, the bit fields come out as 'true' or 'false.'  While that is what they are being used as, this then creates issues later.  When I try to load them into the PG DB (again, via Flask-Fixtures), I get an exception of:
```
sqlalchemy.exc.ProgrammingError: (psycopg2.ProgrammingError) column "isActive" is of type bit but expression is of type boolean
```
If I change the true/false entries to 1/0 in the Yaml file, I then get:
```
sqlalchemy.exc.ProgrammingError: (psycopg2.ProgrammingError) column "isActive" is of type bit but expression is of type integer
```
Is there a way to mark them such that Flask-Fixtures will turn them back into Bit objects before it tries to insert them? Does Flask-Fixtures introspect on the column

I realize the proper answer here is "convert them to boolean fields," but this is a legacy database, and quick-and-dirty changes to the schema would not happen soon. :)

This may well be an issue with SQLAlchemy, as it seems it converts from the bit field to a true/false value when selecting, but then does not convert back when inserting the value.

How *DO* you pass a bit object to SQLAlchemy?

issue_title: subprocess imports (PIPE, Popen, etc).

Django Version: Django 1.11.1
Database and version used: CloudSQL MySQL 5.7
Version: Django-MySQL 2.1.0
Python version: 2.7 (GoogleAppEngine)

When deploying on certain cloud architectures (here Google App Engine), the valid way to use subprocess is to do a global import:

`import subprocess`

`subprocess.PIPE`

issue_title: Admin conflict detection
issue_body:
Here's a slightly odd idea.

Sometimes you have models that are being edited by several users on the admin site. Possibly concurrently. Currently django admin will silently overwrite a model instance, even if the instance was changed by another user since the populated form was "checked out" from the admin app.

What would be nice is if django-reversion could wrap the change form view, adding a hidden form field with the current revision id of the "checked out" form contents. If, when the form was POSTed back the revision id was no longer the latest revision, the admin app could, at its simplest, give the user a warning saying who made the intervening edit(s) and what the comment was, and ask them to confirm the overwriting.

(A more complicated implementation could show the differences between the objects, or maybe even offer to merge them, but that may be going a bit far...)

This may be out of django-reversion's scope, but I think it would fit in quite well with the feature set.


robert.

issue_title: Migration 0003 is extremely slow
issue_body:
On my projects I currently can’t run migration 0003. It’s taking forever, I let it run more than 3 hours in vain. I’m using exclusively PostgreSQL.

That’s because of this query: https://github.com/etianen/django-reversion/blob/v2.0.0/reversion/migrations/0003_auto_20160601_1600.py#L15
It’s not returning distinct results because of [the default ordering of Version](https://github.com/etianen/django-reversion/blob/v2.0.0/reversion/models.py#L99). It’s therefore returning a gigantic list. This list is used to generate ludicrous SQL queries containing thousands of  `OR` (even millions in my case).

The solution is simply to add an empty `.order_by()` at the end of the line.

issue_title: Add dtype keyword to np.eye
clean what is importable from battle_tested with __all__
Incompatible with DRF > 3.0.2
issue_body:
This package is incompatible with Django Rest Framework since version 3.0.3. An import in `relations.py` that drf-nested-routers uses in DRF has been removed and as such it now fails with an ImportError:

```python
from rest_framework.compat import urlparse
```

You can see the change in DRF here:

https://github.com/tomchristie/django-rest-framework/commit/b33a6cbff16e5a28a1a696e2ac617303da181720#diff-ce493f71b3679e91b72126c168670399

I propose this to be fixed by instead using the Django module initially intended to be used:

```python
from django.utils.six.moves.urllib import parse as urlparse
```

I will submit a PR for this, please accept if you agree with my hypothesis and conclusion.

`
import numpy as np
import random

def kMedoids(D, k, tmax=100):
     # determine dimensions of distance matrix D
     m, n = D.shape

    # randomly initialize an array of k medoid indices
    M = np.sort(np.random.choice(n, k))

    # create a copy of the array of medoid indices
    Mnew = np.copy(M)

    # initialize a dictionary to represent clusters
    C = {}
    for t in xrange(tmax):
        # determine clusters, i. e. arrays of data indices
        J = np.argmin(D[:,M], axis=1)
        for kappa in range(k):
            C[kappa] = np.where(J==kappa)[0]
        # update cluster medoids
        for kappa in range(k):
            J = np.mean(D[np.ix_(C[kappa],C[kappa])],axis=1)
            j = np.argmin(J)
            Mnew[kappa] = C[kappa][j]
        np.sort(Mnew)
        # check for convergence
        if np.array_equal(M, Mnew):
            break
        M = np.copy(Mnew)
    else:
        # final update of cluster memberships
        J = np.argmin(D[:,M], axis=1)
        for kappa in range(k):
            C[kappa] = np.where(J==kappa)[0]

    # return results

1. `@one_hot` should be `@cntk.core.Value.one_hot` in [cntk.core.Value.yml](https://github.com/bradygaster/python-sdk-dev/blob/cntk-yaml/python-sdk-dev/docfx_yaml/cntk.core.Value.yml#L51)
2. `@NDArrayView` in [cntk.core.Value.yml](https://github.com/bradygaster/python-sdk-dev/blob/cntk-yaml/python-sdk-dev/docfx_yaml/cntk.core.Value.yml#L126) should be `@cntk.core.NDArrayView` in [cntk.core.NDArrayView.yml](https://github.com/bradygaster/python-sdk-dev/blob/cntk-yaml/python-sdk-dev/docfx_yaml/cntk.core.NDArrayView.yml#L54)
3. `@Value` should be `@cntk.core.Value` in [cntk.core.Value.yml](https://github.com/bradygaster/python-sdk-dev/blob/cntk-yaml/python-sdk-dev/docfx_yaml/cntk.core.Value.yml#L205)

@ericholscher are these above issues in the same pattern? Could we figure out why some cross reference are valid while some are not?

Ideally, ResNet is about 6 times bigger than AlexNet in terms of compute and with a power GPU like Volta or Pascal, I anticipated that the performance difference between the two models to be about 2-3 times or even more. issue_title: Discuss about NDArray::Save and NDArray::Load
issue_body:
There seems to be some mistakes in NDArray::Save(dmlc::Stream *strm) (File: ndarray.cc, Line: 1587~1651) and NDArray::Load(dmlc::Stream *strm) (File:ndarray.cc, Line: 1700~1781). 
    This issue may cause a CUDA: invalid device ordinal with following two steps:
        1. Save a ndarray on gpu(1) in a machine with two gpu card;
        2. Load the ndarray in another machine with only one gpu card the program.

    I find the problem is that in function NDArray::Save the context of ndarray will be saved into stream  and in function   NDArray::Load the data loaded into memory will be converted to the context saved by  NDArray::Save. Why the context is saved in NDArray::Save? To improve portability of saved params, I think it's better to remove these operations to avoid load ndarray in specific device while initializing.

issue_title: SLA Misses not being recorded
issue_body:
Airflow v1.6.1
For our DAGs which run daily we aren't seeing any SLA misses being recorded  when the SLA is missed

I think the issue is here:
```
for ti in max_tis:
    task = dag.get_task(ti.task_id)
    dttm = ti.execution_date
    if task.sla:
        dttm = dag.following_schedule(dttm)
        following_schedule = dag.following_schedule(dttm)
        while dttm < datetime.now():
            if following_schedule + task.sla < datetime.now():
                session.merge(models.SlaMiss(
                    task_id=ti.task_id,
                    dag_id=ti.dag_id,
                    execution_date=dttm,
                    timestamp=ts))
            dttm = dag.following_schedule(dttm)
```

Lets say our DAG runs daily and today is `2016-01-06` (execution_date `2016-01-05T00:00)` and that yesterday `2016-01-05` (execution_date `2016-01-04T00:00`) ran successfully.  This would imply that for today's run, we would have the following walk through:
1. `dttm = 2016-01-05T00:00`
2. `dttm = 2016-01-06T00:00`
3. `following_schedule = 2016-01-07T00:00`
So `following_schedule + task.sla < datetime.now()` will always be false (unless the task takes more than the schedule interval (24 hours) and the SLA time to complete. I think we should instead just have
` if dttm + task.sla < datetime.now():`

issue_title: Hiveserver2 vs beeswax performance
issue_body:
I am looking to upgrade from version 0.9 to 0.12 in order to use ibis. Version 0.12 however, no longer supports beeswax and through some testing I've found that Hiveserver2 performs poorly compared to beeswax. I did testing from 10 rows to a million rows. I found that at 2000 rows, Hiveserver2 takes twice as long to return. For larger queries Hiveserver2 can take up to 5 times as long to return.

I am curious if I am doing something wrong or if there is anything I can do to improve the performance. I'm also wondering what the reasoning for removing beeswax support is. I can't find anything that says hive is deprecating beeswax support.

Here is the code I used to test the performance. When testing ibis I upgraded to 0.12 and when testing without ibis I downgraded to 0.9

```python
import time
import numpy

import sys
import impala.dbapi
import impala

import ibis

def main():
    do_ibis = int(sys.argv[1])
    x = []
    y1 = []
    y2 = []
    y3 = []
    y4 = []
    y5 = []
    y6 = []
    print "fetchall,stddev,conn,stddev,query,stddev"
    for i in xrange(1, 10):
        for j in xrange(1, 10):
            conn = []
            queries = []
            conversions = []
            sql = "select * from database.table t1 limit {}".format(10**i*j)
            for _ in xrange(2):
                if do_ibis:
                    t4 = time.time()
                    con = DeepfieldConnection(host="localhost", port="21000", protocol="beeswax")
                    con = ibis.impala.ImpalaClient(con, hdfs_client=None)
                    t1 = time.time()
                    cursor = con.raw_sql(sql, results=True)
                    t2 = time.time()
                    row = cursor.fetchall()
                    t3 = time.time()
                    cursor.release()
                else:
                    t4 = time.time()
                    con = impala.dbapi.connect(host="localhost", port=21000, protocol="beeswax")
                    cursor = con.cursor()
                    t1 = time.time()
                    cursor.execute(sql)
                    t2 = time.time()
                    for row in cursor:
                        pass
                    t3 = time.time()
                    con.close()
                conn.append(t1 - t4)
                queries.append(t2 - t1)
                conversions.append(t3 - t2)
            x.append(10**i*j)
            y1.append(sum(conversions)/len(conversions))
            y5.append(sum(queries)/len(queries))
            print "{},{},{}".format(x[-1], y5[-1], y1[-1])
            sys.stdout.flush()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

Thanks!

issue_title: Throw more specific exceptions / document attributes
issue_body:
Currently if you were to run code like:

```
# Example from developer manual
# but how do we know this was specifically the `Neo.ClientError.Statement.InvalidSyntax` status code?
try:
    session.run("This will cause a syntax error").consume()
except CypherError:
    raise RuntimeError("Something really bad has happened!")
finally:
    session.close()
```

You would get a `CypherError` which is a catch all for a lot of different errors. Currently you would need to inspect the `code` attribute of the exception to match it up with http://neo4j.com/docs/stable/status-codes.html

Would it make more sense for this driver to check for the various codes and throw a specific error? (in this case maybe `CypherSyntaxError`)

Also, it would be helpful for the docs (or comments in the code itself) to include a mention of `code` and all the attributes we can expect to be set on an exception.

issue_title: Provide a method to apply a RieszRotationMatrix to the output of  RieszFrequencyFilterBankGenerator
issue_body:
[RieszRotationMatrix](https://github.com/InsightSoftwareConsortium/ITKIsotropicWavelets/blob/master/include/itkRieszRotationMatrix.h), as already documented, provides a way to transform an input [physical rotation matrix](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rotation_matrix) into a matrix that can be used to steer riesz components generated from the [RieszFrequencyFilterBankGenerator](https://github.com/InsightSoftwareConsortium/ITKIsotropicWavelets/blob/master/include/itkRieszFrequencyFilterBankGenerator.h).

Please note that the output of the RieszGenerator is a std::vector of output images.
The RieszRotationMatrix is a itkVariableSizeMatrix, so you can get the resulting matrix with rieszRotationMatrix.GetVnlMatrix().

The problem raised by @PierreFontaine is that we do not provide any method to apply the RieszRotationMatrix to the output of the RieszGenerator.

A new method should be provided to do this.

issue_title: S3 module stomps over custom region
issue_body:
While trying to use a custom S3 region, I found that botocore seems to just strip it away, causing us to have slower downloads than usual. Excerpt from our debug logs:

```
2016-01-07 02:12:04,691 - Thread-5 - botocore.handlers - DEBUG - Checking for DNS compatible bucket for: https://s3-us-west-2.amazonaws.com/soma-vault-dev/617edbe0-b4cc-11e5-b9b7-413c7bdf3ab6
2016-01-07 02:12:04,691 - Thread-5 - botocore.handlers - DEBUG - URI updated to: https://soma-vault-dev.s3.amazonaws.com/617edbe0-b4cc-11e5-b9b7-413c7bdf3ab6
```

This seems to be because `fix_s3_host` ignores the region name that's passed in, and uses the global `s3.amazonaws.com` endpoint, which is `us-east-1`.

https://github.com/boto/botocore/blob/develop/botocore/utils.py#L645

We can hack around this programmatically:

```python
import boto3
from botocore.utils import fix_s3_host

s3 = boto3.resource('s3')
s3.meta.client.meta.events.unregister('before-sign.s3', fix_s3_host)
```

But it would be nice if aws-cli and friends could work out of the box.

issue_title: Expose Shutdown Timeout as Configurable Parameter
issue_body:
Currently, when waitress receives a SIGINT or traps a SystemExit exception, it tries to shutdown the running task dispatcher.

The shutdown method embeds inside its function signature a hardcoded timeout value of 5 seconds.

This should not be hidden nor hardcoded.  It should be exposed as a parameter to the operator.

The line of source code I'm talking about is here: https://github.com/Pylons/waitress/blob/68a38e9de560f9538fb8c0eccd6124d0ea393afb/waitress/task.py#L118

and the only place it's called (I believe, outside of tests): https://github.com/Pylons/waitress/blob/68a38e9de560f9538fb8c0eccd6124d0ea393afb/waitress/server.py#L127

Using django-geojson with Django 1.10 throws "''Options' object has no attribute 'get_all_related_objects'".

Following Django 1.10's [migration docs](https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/1.10/ref/models/meta/#migrating-from-the-old-api), [serializes.pt L313](https://github.com/makinacorpus/django-geojson/blob/master/djgeojson/serializers.py#L313-L314) should probably read

```
[f for f in MyModel._meta.get_fields() if (f.one_to_many or f.one_to_one) and f.auto_created and not f.concrete]
```

issue_title: Question: How can I get result types for a Hive query?
issue_body:
`.description` gives:

```
[(u'my_schema.col_one', u'BIGINT_TYPE', None, None, None, None, True),
 (u'my_schema.col_two', u'DATE_TYPE', None, None, None, None, True),
 (u'my_schema.col_three',
  u'BIGINT_TYPE',
  None,
  None,
  None,
  None,
  True)]
```

So the second field gives the types, but I would really like to be able to get at column types I can use in DDL statements.  In this case, BIGINT, DATE, and BIGINT.

**What's the best way to do this?**

I see two options:

1. String manipulation.  Strip `_TYPE` off.
2. Add it to `DBAPITypeObject` by looking it up in `TCLIService.constants.TYPE_NAMES`:

```
for type_id in constants.PRIMITIVE_TYPES:
    name = ttypes.TTypeId._VALUES_TO_NAMES[type_id]
    setattr(sys.modules[__name__], name, DBAPITypeObject([name], constants.TYPE_NAMES[type_id]))
```

Then add `__str__` to `DBAPITypeObject` which returns a valid column name for use in DDL statements.

issue_title: Enhance acceptance tests to cover IPv6
Support configurable timeout with alert related keywords
issue_body:
In S2L 1.8 alert related keywords use hard coded one second wait when looking for alerts. Currently in SL 3.0 beta 3 the timeout has been increased to 5 seconds but is still hard coded. As #917 already proposed, this timeout should be configurable.

We've made a decision that these keywords will use the same timeouts as `Wait ...` keywords by default, but they also have `timeout=None` argument to override.

We also have #933 about cleaning up alerts in general, and that involves deprecating several keywords. The support for custom timeouts should be only added to keywords that are going to be preserved.

issue_title: setuptools 25.1.4 adds extra quotes on LIBPATH
issue_body:
#715's quoting has caused my appveyor builds to start failing with 25.1.4, due to some extra quoting.

With setuptools 25.1.3, builds [succeed](https://ci.appveyor.com/project/minrk/pyzmq/build/master+97/job/b5xoge86se10eh3a), and with 25.1.4 they [fail](https://ci.appveyor.com/project/minrk/pyzmq/build/master+98/job/80o5mu31egb6s946).

The key difference:

on 25.1.3, the link command had args like:

    "/LIBPATH:C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio 14.0\VC\LIB"

on 25.1.4, they look like:

    "/LIBPATH:"C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio 14.0\VC\LIB""

(note the extra quotes).

These are CPython 3.5 x86 builds on appveyor; I don't know what other versions are affected.

issue_title: Proposal for changes in manual offset management
issue_body:
While working on #213 I ran into a confusing quirk, where `held_offsets` may return an offset of -1 (ie we're at the start of the partition), which seems fine until you do the following: 1) store the offsets from `held_offsets` in your application's external offset store, 2) feed them back from there into `reset_offsets`. The problem is that Kafka defines -1 to mean LATEST (it would not have been a problem if LATEST was -2 instead, and EARLIEST was -1). So you may get:

```
In [18]: cons = top.get_simple_consumer(reset_offset_on_start=True, auto_offset_
reset=pykafka.common.OffsetType.EARLIEST)                                       

In [19]: held = cons.held_offsets

In [20]: held
Out[20]: {0: -1, 1: -1, 2: -1}

In [21]: cons.reset_offsets([(cons.partitions[pid], offset) for pid, offset in h
eld.items()])

In [22]: cons.held_offsets
Out[22]: {0: -1, 1: 99, 2: -1}
```

We can avoid the magic numbers altogether by doing something like

 * creating a property `next_offsets` which would just return `held_offsets` + 1, so to speak
 * (plus slap a deprecation warning on `held_offsets`)
 * creating a kwarg `next_offsets` for `reset_offsets()`, which accepts the same as its `partition_offsets` kwarg but expects all values + 1
 * (plus slap a deprecation warning on the `partition_offsets` kwarg)

If that makes sense, I'm happy to write the pullreq.

issue_title: LineModelND assumes optimal fit runs through data center
issue_body:
LineModelND currently imposes a constraint on the resulting fit: that it must pass through the mean of the data.  This additional constraint can result in a sub-optimal fit.

See also: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/47431197/unexpected-behaviour-of-skimage-measure-linemodelnd

issue_title: ``apply_parallel`` slower than (what I) expected
issue_body:
I'm not sure whether this problem qualifies as an issue, but I've been playing with ``util.apply_parallel`` (I badly need this kind of feature for large images), and it turns out that the result is slower than what I expected. Here is a little script that I tried, I just would like to know if other people get the same orders of magnitude as I do, or if there is something wrong with my installation.

```
from skimage import util, filters
import numpy as np
from time import time

l = 5000
im = np.random.random((l, l))

t1 = time()
im_gaussian = filters.gaussian(im, 2)
t2 = time()
print('Gaussian filter computed in %f s' %(t2 - t1))

def wrapped(array):
    return filters.gaussian(array, 2)

t3 = time()
res = util.apply_parallel(wrapped, im, chunks=100)
t4 = time()
print('Gaussian filter computed in %f s with apply_parallel' %(t4 - t3))
```

the result is 
```
In [2]: %run test_dask.py
Gaussian filter computed in 0.857147 s
Gaussian filter computed in 12.149871 s with apply_parallel
```

Therefore, apply_parallel turns out to be more than 10x slower. I run the example on an Ubuntu laptop with 4 cores (as confirmed by multiprocessing.cpu_count()), but I had the impression (using top) that only one core was used. However, even on one core, I did not expect such a large overhead coming from apply_parallel. Am I doing something wrong? Obviously, the fact that there are no examples in the docstring and no section in the user guide does not help. I could contribute to improving the documentation, but first I need to understand what's going on :-).

I'm running scikit-image master, and I installed dask[array] (dask version 0.7.0) using pip install (I was a bit annoyed that a different numpy was installed together with dask...).

issue_title: Partition (sequence) ID support
issue_body:
Github issue corresponding to https://jira.numenta.com/browse/RES-112

We want to be able to pass the sequence ID info throughout the network; this is useful for debugging purposes. The [partitionID in the KNNClassifier|https://github.com/numenta/nupic/blob/master/src/nupic/algorithms/KNNClassifier.py#L359] is supposed to do this, but it's not clear this works. So the task is to first test to see if this is functioning properly. Then we need to pass the sequence (partition) ID number throughout the network. The LanguageSensor already has an output 

["sequenceIdOut"|https://github.com/numenta/nupic.research/blob/master/htmresearch/regions/LanguageSensor.py#L101], but this needs to be linked to the subsequent regions in the network. This requires (i) that these regions (SP, TM, TP, KNN, and CLA) have inputs for "sequenceIdIn", and (ii) that the links are created by the network factory...
i. add to the the spec:
          sequenceIdIn=dict(
            description="Sequence ID",
            dataType='UInt64',
            count=1,
            required=False,
            regionLevel=True,
            isDefaultInput=False,
            requireSplitterMap=False)
ii. add the following in the network factory's linking method:
  network.link(sensorRegionName, currentRegionName, "UniformLink", "",
               srcOutput="sequenceIdOut", destInput="sequenceIdIn")

issue_title: NuPIC Walkthrough notebook's "int" dtype doesn't work on some platforms
issue_body:
In this notebook, we should replace occurences of `dtype="int"` with `dtype="uint32`". https://github.com/numenta/nupic/blob/6924a904644bbb8cdba6ca7a62050cf8456614f8/examples/NuPIC%20Walkthrough.ipynb

You can see we had some integer overflow issues in https://discourse.numenta.org/t/inconsistency-between-spatialpooler-in-nupic-and-nupic-bindings/1549/10?u=mrcslws

Sometimes "int" is mapped to 32-bit, other times it's mapped to 64-bit. The C++ SpatialPooler needs 32-bit integers.

issue_title: Add minSparsity option to KNNClassifierRegion
issue_body: Add minSparsity option to KNNClassifierRegion. This option would only add a vector if its sparsity is >= minSparsity. This is useful in situations where the input sparsity varies greatly and you want to have some control over minSparsity. Default would be to have the same behavior as now, which is to add all vectors (minSparsity=0.0).

issue_title: Serialization of dates breaks save() method
Buildozer fails to run on Arch Linux ASCII codec issues
issue_body:
Hello,

When I pip install buildozer and use it in the command line, the following traceback is given:

`Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/bin/buildozer", line 11, in <module>
    load_entry_point('buildozer==0.34', 'console_scripts', 'buildozer')()
  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/buildozer/scripts/client.py", line 13, in main
    Buildozer().run_command(sys.argv[1:])
  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/buildozer/__init__.py", line 122, in __init__
    self.config.read(filename)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/configparser.py", line 697, in read
    self._read(fp, filename)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/configparser.py", line 1015, in _read
    for lineno, line in enumerate(fp, start=1):
  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/encodings/ascii.py", line 26, in decode
    return codecs.ascii_decode(input, self.errors)[0]
UnicodeDecodeError: 'ascii' codec can't decode byte 0xc2 in position 1652: ordinal not in range(128)
`

However, I noted that buildozer is meant for python2 and modified the the first line of the file in /usr/bin/buildozer from:
#!/usr/bin/python
to:
#!/usr/bin/python2

After this modification, buildozer runs fine. This is because on Arch, the default python interpreter is the python3, not python2 as most linux distributions.

So, one suggestion to avoid versioning problems would be to always point to python2, instead of the default python interpreter of the system. Of course, making buildozer work in python3 would be even better, but maybe that's more work to do.

issue_title: NE3: Refactorization of test submodule
issue_body:
The `tests` submodule needs to be rebuilt to accommodate the changes made relative to NE2, in particular, the closer conformance to the NumPy standards and the greater subset of the Python language supported, in particular assignment.  

With NE3 we have now introduced the idea of a generated ‘autotest’ component as well, which compares individual NumExpr operations and functions to their NumPy equivalents, to ensure that NE3 produces equivalent results to NumPy.  

Also NE3 supports a number of functions in `scipy.special`, however that module is not a dependency of NumExpr (and should not be).  In this case we should use conditional test imports of `scipy.special` and only generate test code for the special functions if it is present.  This will avoid breaking continuous integration while still allowing most end-users to test these functions.  

This issue has high priority.

issue_title: Support for environment variables as default
issue_body:
As I user I'd like to be able to specify some of my arguments as environment variables.

Quite often I have a single bash script performing many calls to a script written using python-fire. Many of the functions defined in the python script contain shared arguments which I am just effectively passing from my environment variables to each of the calls. Having a way to pass those through would be extremely useful.

## Desired behaviour:

Given:
```python3
$ cat run.py
import fire
def function_1(arg1, arg2=1):
    print(arg1, arg2)
fire.Fire(function_1)
```

I'd like:
```bash
$ arg1=arg1 python3.6 run.py -- --use_environment
arg1 1
$ arg1=arg1env arg2=arg2env python3.6 run.py arg1 arg2 -- --use_environment
arg1 arg2
$ arg1=arg1env arg2=arg2env python3.6 run.py arg1 -- --use_environment
arg1 arg2env
$ arg1=arg1env arg2=arg2env python3.6 run.py -- --use_environment
arg1env arg2env
$ arg1=arg1env python3.6 run.py -- --use_environment
arg1env 1
```

This would be extremely useful as then I'd be able to have the following:

```bash
export HOST=mydesiredhost
./machine_manager ping -- --use_environment
./machine_manager shutdown -- --use_environment
# No need to pass --host=mydesiredhost on each command
```

issue_title: Icon request: icon-coloradd-symbol(s)
issue_body:
Hello all!

I have some suggestions for new Accessibility Icons.

ColorADD has come out with universal color symbols to aid those suffering from color-blindness, but these symbols may be used everywhere to help differentiate colors. These could be added onto legends next to pie/bar charts to help those understand the colors. The card game UNO has just started to incorporate the symbols.

More information can be found here:
http://www.coloradd.net/code.asp

The current fa-square and fa-square-o could be used, or similar and larger ones may need to be added, to allow for the stacking of smaller icons inside.
While stacking is almost the idea behind ColorADD, they couldn't be stacked easily in FA. For instance, "purple" and "brown" are more different than just a line between the two wedges.

If we started with:
"fa-coloradd-blue, fa-coloradd-green, fa-coloradd-yellow, fa-coloradd-orange, fa-coloradd-red, fa-coloradd-purple, fa-coloradd-brown"
we would have the seven basic colors.
From there, stacking would work to create the seven color-light and seven color-dark icons. (Lighter colors could be shown stacked with a *new* 'fa-coloradd-white' and darker colors could be shown with 'fa-inverse' and a *new* 'fa-coloradd-black'.

That just leaves creating four more. Two of them (fa-coloradd-grey-light and fa-coloradd-grey-dark) could be made from one smaller-squircle (fa-coloradd-grey?) and just fa-inverse'd and stacked as needed.
The last two gold and silver require creating, in their terms, a parenthesis to the left of a large squircle; one coloradd-white and one coloradd-black. Gold and silver would likely require them being their own call out "fa-coloradd-gold" and "fa-coloradd-silver" because of alignments if we tried to stack icons.

And finally, if this is a duplicate, I am sorry. The Git search and Advanced Search appears to not be all that good at searching for previous suggestions. :(
![code_adicao](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/31778541/30229961-09b7334a-94b2-11e7-86be-628fdf227fa8.gif)
![code_cinzas](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/31778541/30229965-09bf0bd8-94b2-11e7-89fe-f366180dd15a.gif)
![code-claro-escuro](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/31778541/30229964-09bc2f6c-94b2-11e7-96b1-606d46cb7b54.gif)
![codigo](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/31778541/30229963-09b8877c-94b2-11e7-92fa-f62f1ce7ca5b.gif)

issue_title: Issue with Boolean types in code generated by swagger-codegen
issue_body: Here is a minimal (non-)working example needed to reproduce this issue. test.yml: ``` --- swagger: '2.0' info: version: '0' title: 'Boolean Test' paths: /test: get: description: A test endpoint. parameters: - name: truthiness in: query description: A test parameter. type: boolean default: true responses: 204: description: Do some magic. ``` Process this file via swagger-codegen: `java -jar /usr/local/Cellar/swagger-codegen/2.1.5/libexec/swagger-codegen-cli.jar generate -i ./test.yml -l python-flask -o .` Here is the resulting swagger.yml: ``` --- swagger: "2.0" info: version: "0" title: "Boolean Test" paths: /test: get: tags: - "default_controller" description: "A test endpoint." operationId: "controllers.default_controller.test_get" parameters: - name: "truthiness" in: "query" description: "A test parameter." required: false type: "boolean" default: true responses: 204: description: "Do some magic." definitions: {} ``` Running this with `python app.py` results in the following error: ``` Traceback (most recent call last): File "~/.virtualenvs/swagger/lib/python3.5/site-packages/connexion/decorators/validation.py", line 75, in validate_type return make_type(value, param_type) File "~/.virtualenvs/swagger/lib/python3.5/site-packages/connexion/decorators/validation.py", line 53, in make_type return type_func(value) File "~/.virtualenvs/swagger/lib/python3.5/site-packages/connexion/utils.py", line 135, in boolean raise ValueError('Invalid boolean value') ValueError: Invalid boolean value During handling of the above exception, another exception occurred: Traceback (most recent call last): File "~/.virtualenvs/swagger/lib/python3.5/site-packages/connexion/operation.py", line 118, in validate_defaults validation.validate_type(param, param['default'], 'query', param['name']) File "~/.virtualenvs/swagger/lib/python3.5/site-packages/connexion/decorators/validation.py", line 77, in validate_type raise TypeValidationError(param_type, parameter_type, parameter_name) connexion.decorators.validation.TypeValidationError: Wrong type, expected 'boolean' for query parameter 'truthiness' During handling of the above exception, another exception occurred: Traceback (most recent call last): File "app.py", line 8, in app.add_api('swagger.yaml', arguments={'title': ''}) File "~/.virtualenvs/swagger/lib/python3.5/site-packages/connexion/app.py", line 123, in add_api validate_responses=validate_responses) File "~/.virtualenvs/swagger/lib/python3.5/site-packages/connexion/api.py", line 95, in __init__ self.add_paths() File "~/.virtualenvs/swagger/lib/python3.5/site-packages/connexion/api.py", line 136, in add_paths self.add_operation(method, path, endpoint) File "~/.virtualenvs/swagger/lib/python3.5/site-packages/connexion/api.py", line 117, in add_operation validate_responses=self.validate_responses, resolver=self.resolver) File "~/.virtualenvs/swagger/lib/python3.5/site-packages/connexion/operation.py", line 103, in __init__ self.validate_defaults() File "~/.virtualenvs/swagger/lib/python3.5/site-packages/connexion/operation.py", line 122, in validate_defaults param_type=param['type'])) connexion.exceptions.InvalidSpecification: ``` Manually changing `boolean` in swagger.yml to `bool` or `Boolean` causes the script to run without error, but then the datatype in the Swagger UI is incorrect: Finally, changing `boolean` to `bool` or `Boolean` in the original test.yml causes the following error with swagger-codegen: ``` reading from ./test.yml [main] INFO io.swagger.codegen.DefaultCodegen - generated operationId testGet for Path: get /test [main] WARN io.swagger.codegen.DefaultCodegen - Type is NULL for Serializable Parameter: io.swagger.models.parameters.QueryParameter@795a5d7b [main] WARN io.swagger.codegen.DefaultCodegen - warning! Property type "null" not found for parameter "truthiness", using String writing file ./swagger/swagger.yaml writing file ./app.py writing file ./README.md ``` I was also going to open an issue with swagger-codegen, but given the fact that altering the name of the type messes up its representation in the Swagger UI indicates to me that the problem is likely on connexion's end.

issue_title: CMAKE_MODULE_PATH:  Remove rule?
issue_body:
Faithful to the ROS ["CMake coding standards"][1], in `src/catkin_lint/checks/misc.py`, `CMAKE_MODULE_PATH` is [included][2] in the `critical_vars`, which lists CMake variables that should not be overwritten.

Over in ros/catkin#950, there are a few suggestion for how to handle package-local CMake modules while adhering to those coding standards.  @dirk-thomas suggested the standard simply be removed and accordingly submitted PR ros/catkin#951.

This is a heads-up to this repo's followers about the proposed change.

[1]: https://github.com/ros/catkin/blob/kinetic-devel/doc/user_guide/standards.rst

issue_title: What are the roles of the various branches?
issue_body:
Hi Python-Jira team!

I'm trying to work through a tricky bug in {{python-jira}} with regards to a workaround that's been implemented to circumvent Atlassian Jira's https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRA-41559 "Some REST calls return 200 with no body and AUTHENTICATED_FAILED" bug.

To that effect I'm trying to understand what the differences are between the various branches and tags on this project, to be able to base my work off the most up to date version of the project.

I assume `RELEASE == 1.0.7 < master < develop`, but would love to see that documented somewhere. Should I start a github wiki page, or would that more naturally be documented in the `Contributing` section in the doc? Or am I perhaps missing part of the doc?

Also would it be possible to explicitly tag the `1.0.7` release tag on github to make it more explicit?
Thanks!

cc @ssbarnea

issue_title: package_meta tries to run git on module load (also, fails in python 2.7 if "git branch" fails)
issue_body:
I'm surprised that importing the jira client now runs `git branch` in a subprocess seemingly irrespective of being in development or not. This seems like a high performance cost to be paying at module import when not running in development. This is the case on the `develop` branch.

Also, `run_but_ignore_errors` fails in python 2.7 when there is an error in the `try` section:

```
        try:
            return subprocess.check_output(*args, shell=True, stderr=devnull,
                                           **kwargs)
        except (subprocess.SubprocessError,
                FileNotFoundError, OSError):
            return None
```

because `subprocess.SubprocessError` was introduced in Python 3.3: `AttributeError: 'module' object has no attribute 'SubprocessError'`.

issue_title: Picking and setting values of tensors at particular batch
issue_body:
Hi,

It would be nice that `pick` could support retrieving values from a specific batch. Similarly, we could have a `set` operation to allow setting values of particular rows/columns/batches. This is extremely useful to allow for mixed batching/unbatching operations. E.g.,

```
1. Batched LSTM encoding of inputs
2. Use `pick` to get encoding results for each batch. Build operations for each batch dynamically.
3. Use `set` to merge data from different batches and continue with batched operation for efficiency.
```

issue_title: Solution for large ids by adding O(1) lookup
issue_body:
In your documentation it reads:

> Using Redis bitmaps you can store events for millions of users in a very little amount of memory (megabytes). You should be careful about using huge ids (e.g. 2^32 or bigger) as this could require larger amounts of memory.

It might be a potential solution to create a hash table that keeps track of huge ids and maps them back down to smaller indexes.

For example, a user with an id of 192329230202 could be mapped to a smaller index 1 in the bitmap. This would require an O(1) lookup before a `SETBIT' so it shouldn't affect time performance, but it would require more space on disk. 

Steps to implement:

1. A new user performs an signup event. 
1. Find the users_counter for the current day `GET "users_counter:20160614"` which would respond back with something like 2. 
1. Add new `User(id:192329230202)` to the `user_index` table and reassign to `User(internal_id:3)`. This would do something like `SET "users_index:20160614:192329230202" 3`
1. Once this is completed successfully you would want to run `INCR users_count:20160614`
1. `SETBIT "events:search:20160614" 3 1` bit into feature signup at `User(3)` index instead of at the end of the bitmap which requires creating and storing empty bits. 

This would most likely add complexity into how your query the data and you would have to store a reference to lookup each user. In my proposal I used a different lookup per day to reset the bitmap indexes everyday, but this might be more trouble than just maintaining one large ongoing table.

Let me know your thoughts and if this something you are interested in promoting into an enhancement.

issue_title: Running and bundling tests with namespace packages
issue_body:
(Context: PR #2223 towards #1978)

PR #2223's tests can't run because nose will place `cwd` into `sys.path` when running tests. This means `import google.cloud` will import `./google/cloud` instead of `{env}/lib/python{x}.{y}/site-packages/google/cloud`. This will clobber all other `google` namespace packages. There is an long-open issue [here](https://github.com/nose-devs/nose/issues/856), note that Nose has been largely abandoned.

There are a two of ways we could resolve this, and maybe we should do both:

1. Move the tests to their own top-level package as described in the [py.test docs](http://doc.pytest.org/en/latest/goodpractices.html#choosing-a-test-layout-import-rules).
1. Switch to py.test's test runner, `pytest`. It's fully compatible with nose/unittest style tests. py.test appropriately deals with namespace/installed packages.

## On switching to py.test

1. py.test has slightly different arguments than nose, notably for coverage. This is pretty easy to deal with.
1. Switching to py.test will either mean we have to switch to having the tests in a separate package *or* that we have to switch from tox to [nox](https://nox.readthedocs.io). The reason is that py.test will try to import relative to the test modules it discovers, and so will try to import the module-under-test from the current working directory, but will actually give an error because the same module is present in `site-packages`. This can be resolved by either moving the tests to a separate package or installing the package in "editable" mode with `pip install -e`. Unfortunately, tox doesn't easily allow you to change the command used to install the package under test. There might be a few hacks (turning off sdist and manually installing the package). Nox however gives you complete control over the creation, [here's a simple config](https://gist.github.com/jonparrott/0b2873711e49ab07fdac59b9c5e640c6) that works for gcloud-python.

## On moving the tests to a test package

The were originally concerns about having the tests in a separate top-level tests package because we didn't want it to get installed with gcloud-python but we did want to distribute the tests. This should be resolvable by telling `find_packages()` to skip `tests` and including `tests` in `manifest.in`. This means that wheels and sdists will contain the tests folder for downstream packagers, but pip will not install the tests package into `site-packages`.

issue_title: How should we represent 'geoValue' field values in Search?
issue_body:
The [Search docs for `geoValue`](https://cloud.google.com/search/reference/rest/google/cloudsearch/v1/FieldValue#fields.oneof_value.google.cloudsearch.v1.FieldValue.geo_value) don't specify acceptable formats:  they link to a Wiki page of what is basically all known ways humans have ever spelled latitude and longitude.

Strawman: we expect a tuple of two floats, where positive/negative represent degrees North / South of the equator (for latitude) and  East / West of Greenwich (for longitude).  Or else we take any string, require the user to pass in a `field_type` argument to use `geoValue`, and just let the server barf if it doesn't like the string.

issue_title: Logging in GKE is not using the ContainerEngineHandler
issue_labels: [{'id': 311535631, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/api:%20logging', 'name': 'api: logging', 'color': 'f8f8f8', 'default': False}, {'id': 544604243, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/priority:%20p2', 'name': 'priority: p2', 'color': 'fef2c0', 'default': False}, {'id': 77438580, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/type:%20bug', 'name': 'type: bug', 'color': 'd93f0b', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
The `KUBERNETES_SERVICE` environment variable is not set in a pod running on GKE, so `ContainerEngineHandler` never actually gets selected as the default handler [here](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/master/logging/google/cloud/logging/client.py#L307)


This means all the logs go directly to the "Global" section of the Logging tab in the GCloud console, instead of being parsed by the fluentd pod running on the host, and being routed to the right "GKE > *cluster* > *container name*" section. And of course no pod/host/… metadata is added to the log.

I think the checked variable should read `KUBERNETES_SERVICE_HOST` <del>but i did not open a pull request because `ContainerEngineHandler` does not work either: When I manually add a `ContainerEngineHandler` handler to the logging configuration, the logging interface at console.cloud.google.com does not actually parse the JSON that is written to stderr, so the severity for instance is not set correctly (the JSON produced by `ContainerEngineHandler` is directly displayed as the `textPayload`).</del>


And finally (not really related to this but I'm not sure where this should go): the `fluentd-gcp` pod that is running by default on GKE clusters is configured with this fluentd configuration:
```
<filter …>
  …
  format /^(?<severity>\w)(?<time>\d{4} [^\s]*)\s+(?<pid>\d+)\s+(?<source>[^ \]]+)\] (?<log>.*)/
</filter>
```
which is a very weird regex:
 - it parses only one char for the `severity`
 - the `time` is parsed as `YYYY` followed by a space, followed by anything _but_ a space: this does not correspond to any datetime format i can think of
 - the `source` attribute is followed by a `]`, but no matching `[` is in the regex

The `severity` issue makes it impossible to actually use this filter since stackdriver does not accept logs with a severity outside the usual `INFO|WARN|ERROR|…`


--

All-in-all, this makes it really hard to log data from a python pod: logging with the `CloudLogging` handler goes to "Global" by default (and is missing many kubernetes-related meta), <del>logging to stderr a JSON-formatted object is not parsed by the console.cloud UI,</del> and the regex in the fluentd setup cannot pick up any valid input

```
OS: Kubernetes 1.6.6, running on GKE with cos
Python version: 3.6 or pypy 5.8
google-cloud-python version: 1.1.0
```

issue_title: Core Exception Rename for 308?
issue_body:
We [currently][1] name our 308 exception `ResumeIncomplete` because that is how Google Cloud Storage resumable uploads [use the status code][3].

But the [RFC][2] officially defines it as `308 Permanent Redirect`. Seems like people [are mad][4] about this?

Also, in Python through 3.4, 308 isn't even around

```python
$ python2.7
Python 2.7.13 (default, Jan  5 2017, 18:58:25) 
[GCC 5.4.0 20160609] on linux2
Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.
>>> import httplib
>>> import six
>>> six.moves.http_client is httplib
True
>>> httplib.responses[200]
'OK'
>>> httplib.responses[308]
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
KeyError: 308
$ python3.4
Python 3.4.5 (default, Jan  5 2017, 19:30:46) 
[GCC 5.4.0 20160609] on linux
Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.
>>> import http.client
>>> import six
>>> six.moves.http_client is http.client
True
>>> http.client.responses[200]
'OK'
>>> http.client.responses[308]
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
KeyError: 308
```

but it made it's way into Python 3.5:

```python
$ python3.5
Python 3.5.2 (default, Jan  5 2017, 19:32:37) 
[GCC 5.4.0 20160609] on linux
Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.
>>> import http.client
>>> import six
>>> six.moves.http_client is http.client
True
>>> http.client.responses[200]
'OK'
>>> http.client.responses[308]
'Permanent Redirect'
```

[1]: https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/29d18bc837b39fd74445b57c5eb55965fded78b3/core/google/cloud/exceptions.py#L97-L99
[2]: https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7538#section-3
[3]: https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/json_api/v1/how-tos/resumable-upload#example_uploading_the_file
[4]: http://insanecoding.blogspot.co.il/2014/02/http-308-incompetence-expected.html

issue_title: Pub/sub: amount of streaming pull operations way higher than streaming ack operations
issue_labels: [{'id': 139685153, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/api:%20pubsub', 'name': 'api: pubsub', 'color': 'f8f8f8', 'default': False}, {'id': 778245707, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/status:%20awaiting%20information', 'name': 'status: awaiting information', 'color': 'fef2c0', 'default': False}, {'id': 810248285, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/triaged%20for%20GA', 'name': 'triaged for GA', 'color': 'b5a1ea', 'default': False}, {'id': 77438584, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/type:%20question', 'name': 'type: question', 'color': 'c5def5', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
We are using `python 2.7.12` with `google-cloud-pubsub==0.30.1` and `grpcio==1.9.1` and have a very basic subscriber script:

    import random
    import time

    from google.cloud import pubsub

    GCLOUD_PROJECT_ID = <our_project_id>
    SUBSCRIPTION_NAME = <subscription_name>

    def pubsub_message_callback(message):
        time.sleep(random.random())
        message.ack()

    def main():
        sub_client = pubsub.SubscriberClient()
        full_subscription_name = 'projects/{project_id}/subscriptions/{subscription}'.format(
            project_id=GCLOUD_PROJECT_ID,
            subscription=SUBSCRIPTION_NAME,
        )
        pubsub_subscription = sub_client.subscribe(
            full_subscription_name,
            flow_control=pubsub.types.FlowControl(max_messages=1)
        )
        pubsub_subscription.open(
            pubsub_message_callback
        )
        while True:
            pass

    if __name__ == '__main__':
        main()

When having a large backlog and running this script for 20 minutes, the amount of streaming pull operations is 100 times the amount of streaming ack operations. [(see image)](https://i.stack.imgur.com/rFa7l.png)

As the the flow control is set to `max_messages=1` I would expect that it will always wait to ack the received message, before receiving another one. And even when flow control was not set to `max_messages=1` it still looks like unintended behaviour as the total amount of pull operations is still way higher than the amount of ack operations (so what happens with those received/unacked messages?). Is this a bug or is it intended behaviour?

Also asked this as a question on [stackoverflow](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/48640525/gcp-pub-sub-python-client-amount-of-streaming-pull-messages-way-higher-than-str)

issue_labels: [{'id': 77676109, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/api:%20datastore', 'name': 'api: datastore', 'color': 'd4af37', 'default': False}, {'id': 544604243, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/priority:%20p2+', 'name': 'priority: p2+', 'color': 'ffee58', 'default': False}, {'id': 77438580, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/type:%20bug', 'name': 'type: bug', 'color': 'db4437', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
We've recently started to see more BrokenPipeErrors while using gcloud-python.  We started noticing them more when we upgraded to Kubernetes 1.2.4 (vs 1.1), if that makes any difference.  But, I think this was just a coincidence.

Anyway, I delved deeper and saw that the dev datastore version was sending FIN ACK, and then RST ACK packets after some inactivity.  The datastore client machine sees these packets as well.  The next time the client tries to do a query against datastore, we get a broken pipe error.  It would be nice if the gcloud-python / httplib2 did some pre-emptive checking to see if the connection was closed retried the query itself.  

Some code snippets:

This is one way to detect if the socket on the other side has closed a connection.

```
  possibly_disconnected = False
    data_waiting = bytes() 
    try: 
        data_waiting = conn.recv(BUFFER_SIZE, socket.MSG_DONTWAIT|socket.MSG_PEEK) 
    except BlockingIOError: 
        # There is no data available... and the socket still seems good 
        pass 
    else: 
        possibly_disconnected = len(data_waiting) == 0
```

Here is a stack trace of  the exception

issue_title: Pants build or pants py on python_binary that uses unittest results in zero tests being run
issue_body:
I'm seeing odd behavior when comparing the output of running a simple Python unittest script in the shell to the same script being invoked through Pants.  With the 0.0.24 version of pants, running `pants py target`, or running PEX generated through `pants build target`, the test below will not run.  When the Python script invoked through the shell, the test will run.  

    $ cat test_simple.py 
    import unittest 
    class SimpleTest(unittest.TestCase): 
      def test_fail(self): 
      self.failUnless(True) 
    if __name__ == '__main__': 
      unittest.main()

    $ cat BUILD 
    python_binary( 
      name = "test_simple", 
      source = "test_simple.py",
    )

    $ python test_simple.py 
    . 
    ---------------------------------------------------------------------- 
    Ran 1 test in 0.000s 
    OK 

    $ ./pants py path/to/dir_with_build_file:test_simple 
    /path/to/pants_root path/to/dir_with_build_file:test_simple 
    ---------------------------------------------------------------------- 
    Ran 0 tests in 0.000s 
    OK
    # running pex generated through pants build
    $ ./dist/test_simple.pex 
    ---------------------------------------------------------------------- 
    Ran 0 tests in 0.000s 
    OK

After talking to a contributor, he suggested that I open this issue as it may be a bug in Pants.
I've tried the above using Python2.7.8 and Python2.6.9 on OSX 10.10.1, with no difference in the result.
Please let me know if you require additional information.  Thank you.

issue_title: Difference in Accuracies between scikit-learn and pmml models
issue_body:
Hi, this is a follow up to [https://github.com/jpmml/sklearn2pmml/issues/65](url).

As described, I have two models (both Linear SVMs with 5000 features) one created using scikit learn's pipeline and one created using sklearn2pmml pipeline. Both models are multi-class models (# classes is around 13) and are trained on the same set of data with the same set of parameters. Evaluating them on the same set of data has resulted in different accuracies. The difference in precision between the two models is 24% for one class and 5% for some other class. Recall is similar.  Is this behavior expected?

issue_title: unidecode is GPL
issue_body:
Just FYI: `Unidecode >= 0.4.17` listed in install_requires is GPL, so parserator can't be MIT. 
Options: use some earlier Unidecode version (it used not to be GPL); use https://github.com/kmike/text-unidecode

issue_body:
## Summary

Not sure if this is already being handled elsewhere? But I think it's important for early UX, especially anyone trying to test Kolibri to get an early impression.

Before downloading a content channel, users should be presented with data about the channel:

1. Some description
1. Figures, like available video count
1. Total data needed to be transferred
1. Total drive space required

During the download:

1. Total size MB
1. Currently downloaded MB
1. Transfer speed avg (past 10 minutes?)
1. ETA

We should of course handle application crashes (device reboots), and connection outages.

issue_title: Request - Shortcuts for creating folders with descriptions (or bug where create_folder doesn't allow slashes?)
issue_body:
I'm trying to update new versions of public datasets and would like to make it easier/more automated in the future. Right now I add descriptions for each version in the UI but ideally I'd be able to just add automated descriptions to the folders as they're created. This is fairly easy for new datasets, but pretty awkward for new folders.

To create a specific folder with a description, I have to first specify the vault, then make a new folder, then make a new folder within that folder with the description, then concatenate all the paths together to make the dataset path and then create the dataset.
I guess alternatively I can create a dataset from a path, then find the folder that's auto-created and then give that a description (which isn't so bad).

my initial code was
```
    vault_path = 'solvebio:blahblah'
    vault = solvebio.Vault.get_by_full_path(vault_path)

    folder_path = 'SomeDataset/SomeSchemaVersion-' + data_dump_version
    folder_description = data_dump_version + ' version downloaded from some url'
    vault.create_folder(folder_path, description=folder_description)

    dataset_full_path = ''.join(
        vault_path,
        ':/',
        folder_path,
        '/',
        dataset_type.title()
    )

    # create a large SolveBio Dataset
    dataset = solvebio.Dataset.get_or_create_by_full_path(
        dataset_full_path,
        fields=tpl_json['fields'],
        capacity='large',
        description=metadata['dataset_type']
    )
```
but this creates the error
`SolveError:  {u'field_errors': [{u'field': u'filename', u'message': [u'Filename cannot contain slashes']}]}`

because my folder path has slashes in it.

anyways, if someone has a shorter way of just creating a folder with a description and then putting a dataset into it, that would be great

issue_title: Ability to have girder_worker copy the whole parent item for input file parameters
issue_body:
Issue
-------
This issue concerns the development of a mechanism to instruct girder_worker to optionally download/copy the whole parent item of input file parameters into the docker container.

Background
----------------
I faced this need while developing an [infrastructure](https://github.com/DigitalSlideArchive/HistomicsTK/blob/master/server/rest_slicer_cli.py#L809) to automatically generate REST end-points for slicer execution model CLIs. A large part of this infrastructure is the conversion from slicer execution model xml spec to girder_worker task spec along with the input/output bindings.

For girder_worker tasks where one of the inputs is a girder item,  if the item contains a single file then girder_worker will copy the file to the worker machine and provide a path of this file to the docker task. Instead, if the input girder item contains multiple files, then girder_worker will copy all files of the item into a newly created directory on the worker machine and give the path of the this directory to the docker task. 

With image files, the following two scenarios occur:
* **Image information spanning multiple files:** This is the case with dicom series images wherein the header information is stored in the `.mhd` file and image data is stored in dicom files located adjacent to the .mhd file. 
* **Image information is in a single file:** This is the case with images stored in `.mha`, `.nrrd`, and `.png` formats wherein both the header and image data is stored in a single file. 

To support both the aforementioned scenarios, I would like to map input parameters of type image in slicer xml spec to a file on girder and have an ability to instruct girder_worker to copy the whole parent item into a directory on the worker machine and get a path to the specified file within it for use in the docker task.
